title: Lizard Warrior
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Otheruses|Warrior}} {{Species |image=[[File:Lizard_Warriors_with_capturing_sticks.png]] |name=Lizard Warrior |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]][[Ewoks (1985) 4|''Ewoks'' (1985) 4]] |class=[[Reptile/Legends|Reptile]] |subspecies= |races= |height=Over 1.2 [[Meter/Legends|meter]][[Ewoks (1985) 4|''Ewoks'' (1985) 4]] shows that the Lizard Warriors stood slightly taller than the adult Ewok [[Graak (lawbreaker)/Legends|Graak]]. According to ''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]'' adult Ewoks averaged 1.2 meters in height. |length=Over 2.4 meters[[Ewoks (1985) 4|''Ewoks'' (1985) 4]] shows that the Lizard Warriors are roughly twice as long as they are tall. ''Ewoks 4: Valley of Evil'' also shows that the Lizard Warriors stood slightly taller than the adult Ewok [[Graak (lawbreaker)/Legends|Graak]]. According to ''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]'' adult Ewoks averaged 1.2 meters in height. |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor=[[Color/Legends|Green]] with yellow underbelly |haircolor= |feathers= |eyecolor=Yellow, brown[[Star Wars (1977) 92|''Star Wars'' (1977) 92]] |distinctions=Long [[Nose/Legends|snout]] and tail, spikes |lifespan= |origin= |habitat= |diet= |language= }} {{Quote|Curse them! Those scouts are far more cunning than I suspected, Joddar!''"
"''Do not fear, Graak! It's time for your best pupil to join the hunt! And I will stop them… or die trying!|Graak and Joddar intensify a most dangerous game|}} The '''Lizard Warriors''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Reptile/Legends|reptilians]] who lived in a [[Desert/Legends|desert]] region of the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. Members of the species had [[Color/Legends|green]] scales and yellow underhides with yellow spikes running down the spine. Their bodies were [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] except for a long tail. Lizard Warrior culture valued both martial prowess and [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] skill, and they marked the borders of their territory with [[totem pole]]s. Sometime before [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], an [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] named [[Graak (lawbreaker)/Legends|Graak]] was exiled to the [[Valley of the Lizard Warriors]]. When he taught his advanced martial skills to the reptilians, they selected him as their new leader. Graak instigated a new system whereby strength and fighting prowess were valued over other attributes. In 3 ABY, the Lizard Warriors captured two trespassing Ewoks, [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]] and [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket W. Warrick]], and made them the quarry of a species-wide hunt. Nevertheless, when [[Joddar]], Graak's lead warrior, was saved by the pair, he promised to escort them out of the valley. Graak attempted to prevent their escape, so Joddar overthrew him and assumed leadership of his people. The [[4 ABY/Legends|following year]], the Lizard Warrior [[Dracos]] represented his people among the members of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] at [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]], home of the [[Bright Tree Village tribe|tribe]] of Ewoks who had helped the Alliance overthrow the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Dracos trained in military combat for a [[Time/Legends|time]] under a member of the Alliance, the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. ==Biology and appearance== {{Quote|Who are these guys, Wicket?''"
"''I don't know… but if there was a contest for ugliness, they'd sure win first prize!|Teebo and Wicket W. Warrick meet the Lizard Warriors|}} [[File:Lizard_Warrior_archers.png|thumb|left|250px|Lizard Warriors had long tails that lay on the ground when they stood.]] Lizard Warriors were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] bipedal [[Reptile/Legends|reptiles]] whose physiology was largely [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]]. The exception was a long tail, which began thick at its base and tapered to a point. The tail dragged the ground when the reptilians walked or stood, but it projected straight behind when they ran. The [[Species/Legends|species]]' short [[Limb/Legends|legs]] and stooped posture made them stand over 1.2 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall, making them slightly taller than an [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]]. Likewise, they were roughly twice as long as they were tall at around 2.4 meters in length. Nevertheless, at full height, some Lizard Warriors stood taller than an average [[Human/Legends|Human]]. Their digits sported sharp claws, four fingers per [[Limb/Legends|hand]] and three toes per foot. Lizard Warriors were covered in [[Color/Legends|green]] scales with a yellow underbelly. Nevertheless, their hides offered little protection from punctures from such hazards as [[burr ball]]s. Many Lizard Warriors had a row of yellow spikes running from the crown of the head to the tip of the tail, while others had two isolated spikes toward the back of the head. An additional spike grew from each elbow. The bulk of the Lizard Warrior head was given over to a long, pointed snout full of sharp [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]]. Two [[Nose/Legends|nostril holes]] pierced this muzzle at its farthest extremity, and a red tongue lay within. A pair of narrow, yellow or brown [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] on the sides of the head flanked the snout at its base. At least some Ewoks found the Lizard Warriors' appearance unattractive. ==Society and culture== {{Quote|Well, you have won the game and survived the hunt! The totem pole on that rise marks the end of our valley!|Joddar, after being saved by Teebo and Wicket W. Warrick|}} [[File:Lizard_Warrior_totem_pole.png|thumb|right|upright|250px|Lizard Warriors marked their territory with totem poles.]] A group of Lizard Warriors lived in a [[Desert/Legends|desert]] valley on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]], where they kept to themselves and rarely interacted with other species.{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/nagai11202009/index.html|text=The Forgotten War: The Nagai and the Tofs|int=The Forgotten War: The Nagai and the Tofs}} Their region, named the [[Valley of the Lizard Warriors]] after its inhabitants, lay several [[Standard day/Legends|days]]' journey from the [[Tree/Legends|forests]] of [[Happy Grove/Legends|Happy Grove]]. Thus, the Ewoks of [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]] who lived there had little knowledge of the species. Nevertheless, Endor's Lizard Warriors spoke [[Ewokese/Legends|Ewokese]]. The Lizard Warriors had a martial culture that valued warfare and [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]]. The pursuit of game was valued not only as a necessity of life, but as a sport. The species went to battle armed with relatively primitive weapons, such as recurve [[Bow/Legends|bows]], wooden [[Arrow/Legends|arrows]], and throwing [[Spear/Legends|spears]] with [[Stone/Legends|stone]] heads. Still, some Lizard Warriors had access to more sophisticated arms, like metal spear points and [[Sword/Legends|swords]]. What little clothing they wore was devoted to warfare: a quiver on the back for carrying arrows or a belt around the waist for holding a sword. The species followed the greatest warrior among them. Such an individual did not necessarily have to be a member of the species; in fact, in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]'' places the [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']] animated television series in 3.5 ABY but makes no mention of the [[Star Wars: Ewoks (1985)|''Ewoks'']] comic book series. Nevertheless, as the character designs in the comic book are identical to those of the the first season of the ''Ewoks'' television show, the comic book stories must take place around the same time as the animated cartoon. Endor's Lizard Warriors were ruled by an Ewok named [[Graak (lawbreaker)/Legends|Graak]]. Lizard Warriors adhered to a strict code of [[honor]] that compelled them to keep their word and repay any act of kindness. The Lizard Warriors were territorial and demarcated their lands with [[totem pole]]s: carved wooden posts decorated with such items as cloth, crossed sticks, feathers, [[Bird/Legends|bird]] wings, and [[Skeleton/Legends|skulls]]. If Lizard Warriors detected strangers in their lands, they ambushed the interlopers from behind rocks and [[Bush/Legends|brush]]. The warriors used a special [[capturing stick]] made of a length of wood with a looped cord attached to snag the intruders. Members of the species lived inside caves, the outsides of which were littered with bones. They had access to goods such as bowls, [[Fruit/Legends|fruits]], and jugs of liquid. ==History== {{Quote|That's when I met Joddar and his men and taught them new, deadly fighting skills! In gratitude they asked me to lead them!|Graak|}} [[File:Graak_and_the_Lizard_Warriors.png|thumb|left|250px|In 3 ABY, an Ewok named Graak was the leader of the Lizard Warriors.]] The Lizard Warriors of Endor settled in a desert valley and set up their homes in caves there. At some point, they came into contact with the [[Moon/Legends|moon's]] native Ewoks, who named their territory the Valley of the Lizard Warriors after its reptilian denizens. The warrior [[Joddar]] eventually attained a leadership position in the culture. Years before 3 ABY, a one-eyed Ewok named Graak entered Lizard Warrior territory. He had been exiled from his home, [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]], by the village [[Chief/Legends|chief]], [[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]]. After he demonstrated his skill at combat and promised to train the Lizard Warriors in his techniques, Joddar stepped down to allow Graak to assume leadership with Joddar as second-in-command. Under the exile's leadership, the Lizard Warriors adopted a philosophy of survival of the fittest. In 3 ABY, the Lizard Warriors detected two Ewoks—[[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]] and [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket W. Warrick]]—wandering through their territory. They ambushed the interlopers, captured them, and brought them to their caves. Graak made the tresspassers a deal: if they could reach the edge of the Valley of the Lizard Warriors alive, they could go free. However, they would have to escape the Lizard Warriors, who would hunt them down for sport. [[File:Dracos.jpg|right|thumb|upright|180px|Dracos represented the Lizard Warriors during the early days of the Alliance of Free Planets on Endor.]] Several low-level warriors pursued and confronted the Ewoks, but their prey survived. Joddar finally joined the hunt and tracked the Ewoks to a [[Swamp/Legends|swamp]] at the border of the valley. Before he could kill them, however, Joddar came under attack himself from an [[Endorian swamp beast]]. Warrick chased the beast away with a well-aimed [[Bee/Legends|beehive]] and pulled Joddar from the swamp, an act of mercy that saved the Lizard Warrior's life and defied his expectations. Though confused, Joddar thanked his benefactors and swore them his life. The Ewoks asked only to be led to the edge of the swamp and out of Lizard Warrior territory, and Joddar gladly complied. However, Graak arrived on the scene and refused to let the younger Ewoks go. When he attacked and nearly killed Warrick, Joddar stepped in and stopped him. Joddar rejected Graak's protestations of supremacy and explained that the young Ewoks had taught him compassion, something Graak never could understand. He then ousted Graak from his position and assumed leadership of his people once again. Joddar and his Lizard Warriors parted with the younger Ewoks on good terms; Graak, on the other hand, severed ties with his former underlings and cast off into the desert alone. At the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] defeated the forces of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] thanks in large part to the help of a [[Bright Tree Village tribe|tribe]] of Ewok allies on Endor.{{Film|VI}} The Lizard Warriors sent a representative named [[Dracos]] to join the Alliance forces gathered at the Ewok [[City/Legends|settlement]] of Bright Tree Village. Dracos was present as the Alliance leaders formed the [[Alliance of Free Planets]]. [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] later assigned Dracos to train with other Alliance recruits under a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]], [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]].{{CSWECite|I|204|Dracos}} The Lizard Warrior and the others hoped to eventually learn how to use [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to become [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. Nevertheless, Skywalker was still haunted by the memories of how his father, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], had fallen to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]], so he refused to teach the recruits anything beyond basic marksmanship, hand-to-hand combat, and meditation. ==Behind the scenes== Writer [[David Manak|Dave Manak]] and illustrator [[Warren Kremer]] introduced the Lizard Warriors as antagonists to the Ewok characters of [[Ewoks (1985) 4|''Ewoks'' 4]]. The comic book was published by [[Marvel Comics]] in November [[1985]]. The character [[Dracos]], who appeared in [[Marvel Comics]] [[Star Wars (1977) 92|''Star Wars'' (1977) 92]] later that year, was identified as a member of the species in the article "[[The Forgotten War: The Nagai and the Tofs]]," written by [[James McFadden]] and published as part of the [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] website in [[2009]]. ''Valley of Evil'' implies that the Valley of the Lizard Warriors was the reptilians' homeland and that, thus, they were native to the Forest Moon. Nevertheless, later sources point to an origin on another unidentified [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. The roleplaying supplement ''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'', for example, names only Ewoks and [[Yuzzum/Legends|Yuzzums]] as native to Endor,''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' and the article "[[Castaways of Endor]]" adds only [[Dulok/Legends|Duloks]] to the list.{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/feature20081201.html|text=Castaways of Endor|int=Castaways of Endor}} According to such works and the roleplaying article "[[Endor and the Moddell Sector]]," the majority of the sentient species who lived on the Forest Moon were survivors of crash landings caused by the main [[Endor (planet)/Legends|planet's]] massive [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] shadow and shield of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] detritus.{{GamerCite|9|Endor and the Moddell Sector}} ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Lizard Warriors}} *[[Ewoks (1985) 4|''Ewoks'' (1985) 4]] {{1st}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 92|''Star Wars'' (1977) 92]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 92}} {{Ret}} ==Sources== *{{CSWECite|I||Dracos}} {{Imo}} *{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/nagai11202009/index.html|text=The Forgotten War: The Nagai and the Tofs|int=The Forgotten War: The Nagai and the Tofs}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Lagarto guerrero }} [[Category:Reptilian sentient species]] [[Category:Sentient species of Endor]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Lizard Warriors]] }}
